name: Flood Risk Mapping using Sentinel-1 SAR
description: Detect inundated areas from Synthetic Aperture Radar (Sentinel-1) imagery combined with DEM analysis for flood extent estimation.
author: CortexGIS

aoi.description: Area of Interest as a GeoJSON polygon or feature collection

slope_threshold.description: Maximum slope (degrees) to include in flood risk area

steps.description: Download Sentinel-1 SAR imagery for the AOI
steps.notes: VV polarization is most sensitive to open water

steps.description: Apply speckle filtering to reduce SAR noise
Convert intensity to dB scale for better interpretability
Threshold SAR imagery to isolate water surfaces (low backscatter)
steps.notes: Threshold -17 dB typical for VV; may vary by incidence angle

steps.description: Retrieve Digital Elevation Model for terrain analysis
Exclude areas with slope > threshold (unlikely to hold water)
Remove noise and close gaps using morphological operations
Convert raster flood mask to vector polygons
Calculate flood extent statistics (area, count of affected pixels)

cot_reasoning: 1. User wants to map flood-affected areas in a region.
2. Sentinel-1 SAR is excellent for this because it penetrates clouds and detects water via low backscatter.
3. Download VV-polarized Sentinel-1 data for August-September (monsoon peak).
4. Preprocess: apply speckle filter to reduce SAR noise (inherent to radar data).
5. Convert to dB scale for easier interpretation and thresholding.
6. Threshold at -17 dB to isolate water bodies (open water has very low backscatter).
7. Fetch DEM to compute slope; exclude steep terrain (slope >2°) as water is unlikely there.
8. Apply morphological closing to remove noise, fill small gaps.
9. Vectorize result to generate polygon boundaries of flooded areas.
10. Compute area statistics for reporting and impact assessment.
caveats: Threshold (-17 dB) is typical but may need adjustment based on local incidence angle.
Requires cloud-free optical validation data for ground truth comparison.
May misclassify calm water bodies (low-backscatter) and some urban areas.
SAR layover and shadow effects in mountainous terrain can affect results.
